name: Caldereta
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Lamb, beef or goat, cut into -1 1/2-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the cubed meat, vinegar, salt, and pepper in a bowl. Set aside for 30 to 40 minutes.
2. Drain the meat, reserving the liquid, and pat it dry.
3. Heat olive oil in a large heavy saucepan over medium heat.
4. Add chiles to the hot oil and stir until they swell and darken (about a few seconds).
5. Remove chiles with a slotted spoon and set aside.
6. Brown the meat cubes in batches, returning them to the pan when browned.
7. Add onion and garlic, cooking for 2 minutes, scraping up juices.
8. Add peppercorns, cinnamon stick, and bay leaves; stir for another minute.
9. Return the meat with its juices, chiles, reserved marinade, and tomato paste to the pan.
10. Stir and cook for 1 minute.
11. Gradually add 2 cups water and bring to a boil.
12. Lower heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes.
13. Peel and cube potatoes; add them when the meat has cooked for 15 minutes, continuing to simmer until the meat is tender (45-60 minutes).
14. Add seeded and strip-cut bell pepper; cook an additional 3-5 minutes before serving.
